title: Vitest coverage in tests/web/virtualization.test.js
description: Comprehensive Vitest suite for the Virtualizer module + recycle pool + filter-indices interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/web/virtualization.test.js exists with >=20 test cases covering: attach/update/detach, visible-window correctness on scroll, recycle-pool size cap, no observer leaks across 100 attaches, filter recompute updates the index list correctly, selection state survives scroll.
Tests run in jsdom (matching the existing Vitest config).
All tests pass in CI; flake rate < 1% over 10 runs.
Test file copies the Virtualizer IIFE verbatim from docs/index.html (matching the existing pattern for xml-processing.test.js and ui-logic.test.js).

steps.details: Set up jsdom container with a known itemHeight (e.g., 72px). Mock window.requestAnimationFrame to fire immediately. Stub IntersectionObserver if needed.
(a) attach with totalCount=100 renders ~visible-window-size cards; (b) update with totalCount=10 collapses; (c) detach removes scroll listener (verify via add/remove count).
Set container.scrollTop = 720 (10 cards down). Trigger scroll handler. Assert visibleStart === 10 (or near).
Render 1000 tracks. Scroll to bottom (scrollTop = 1000 * 72). Count DOM nodes. Assert <= ~50 (40 + buffer slack).
Set selectedTrackIds = new Set([5, 50, 500]). Scroll past index 5. Scroll back. Assert the rendered card for index 5 has class .selected.
Set parsedTracks = 1000 items. Filter to 100. Call Virtualizer.update. Assert totalCount * itemHeight is the new spacer height; visible cards are from the filtered subset.
Spy on IntersectionObserver.observe / unobserve / disconnect. Attach + detach 100 times. Assert .disconnect() is called between each attach.

technicalNotes: Per CLAUDE.md testing approach: JS tests copy functions verbatim from docs/index.html. If a future PR changes the Virtualizer IIFE, the copy in virtualization.test.js must update too.
jsdom doesn't run real layout - mocked dimensions are fine for correctness, but real fps must be verified in playwright.
